# Qora v0.13 ‚Äî complete language reference for AI assistants

Qora is a quantum programming language (Q#/C#-flavored syntax) that compiles to OpenQASM 3.
File extension: `.qor`. This file is the full, authoritative surface spec: everything below is
implemented and everything not mentioned does not exist. When generating Qora code, use ONLY the
constructs listed here.

Docs: https://aj-comp.github.io/Qora/  ¬∑  Repo: https://github.com/AJ-comp/Qora
VS Code extension: `qora-lang.qora-language` (bundles the compiler; errors as you type).

## Compilation model

- One entry file compiles to one OpenQASM 3 program: `qora --json main.qor`.
  No project files. `import` statements load other files (transitively, relative to the importing
  file). Everything merges into one program before name resolution.
- The operation named `Main` (in the global namespace) is the entry point: its body becomes the
  QASM top level. Every other operation becomes a `def`. If no `Main` exists, the first operation
  is the entry.
- Compilation is all-or-nothing: ANY semantic error (codes below) blocks emission and reports every
  violation at once, each with a source span.
- In the emitted QASM every user-defined name gets a trailing `_` (`q` -> `q_`, `Bell` -> `Bell_`,
  `MyLib.Bell` -> `MyLib__Bell_`). This is name mangling for collision safety; do not be surprised
  by it and do not imitate it in source code.

## Program structure

```qora
import gates_lib;               // loads gates_lib.qor next to this file
import lib.gates;               // dots map to directories: lib/gates.qor
import "a b.qor";               // literal relative path form

namespace MyLib {               // optional; may be dotted (namespace A.B { })
    open OtherLib;              // unqualified access to OtherLib's operations (namespace-scope only)
    operation Bell(Qubit[2] q) {
        H(q[0]);
        CNOT(q[0], q[1]);
    }
}

operation Main() {              // global namespace; the entry point; takes NO parameters
    use q = Qubit[2];           // qubit allocation: ONLY in the entry op, top level only
    MyLib.Bell(q);              // qualified call
    bit r = M(q[0]);            // measurement (the only way to get a bit)
    if (r == 1) { X(q[1]); }
}
```

## Types and declarations

- `Qubit` (single qubit), `Qubit[N]` (register, N a literal integer) ‚Äî parameter types only.
- `int`, `bit` ‚Äî classical. `bit` values come ONLY from measurement.
- `use name = Qubit[N];` ‚Äî allocate a register. Entry operation only, not inside loops/branches.
- `const n = expr;` / `const int n = expr;` ‚Äî IMMUTABLE binding (JS-const/Q#-let style): may hold
  any value including a measurement result (`const r = M(q[0]);`), but can never be assigned again
  (QSEM024). `var n = expr;` / `int n = expr;` ‚Äî mutable variable.
- `bit r = M(q[i]);` ‚Äî measure qubit i into a fresh bit. `r = M(q[i]);` re-measures.
  M() is legal ONLY as the whole right-hand side of a declaration/assignment. Never in conditions,
  arguments, or arithmetic (measure into a bit first).
- No return values (operations are void). No calls in expressions. No arrays other than qubit
  registers. Loop bounds and register sizes must be literal numbers.

## Statements

- Gate/operation call: `Name(args);` ‚Äî qubits as `q` (whole register) or `q[i]`.
- `if (cond) { } else { }` (else-if allowed). Conditions: == != < <= > >= && || ! over bits/ints.
- `for i in 0..3 { }` ‚Äî inclusive range, literal bounds.
- `while (cond) { }` ¬∑ `repeat { } until (cond);`
- Comments: `//` to end of line. (No block comments.)

## Built-in gates (complete list; case-sensitive)

- 1-qubit: `H X Y Z S T` ‚Äî e.g. `H(q[0]);`
- 2-qubit: `CNOT CX CY CZ SWAP` ‚Äî e.g. `CNOT(q[0], q[1]);` (CNOT and CX are the same gate)
- 3-qubit: `CCX`
- Rotations (ANGLE FIRST): `Rx Ry Rz` ‚Äî e.g. `Rx(pi/2, q[0]);` Constants: `pi`, `tau`, `euler`.
- Reset: `Reset(q[0]);` `ResetAll(q);` ‚Äî statements, not gates: no functors on them.
- Measurement: `M(q[i])` in the assignment forms above. `Measure`/`measure` are NOT valid.

## Functors

- `Adjoint G(args);` ‚Äî inverse. On a built-in gate -> `inv @`. On a USER operation the compiler
  SYNTHESIZES the inverse def transitively (gates reversed and inverted, for-loops run backwards,
  if-bodies inverted, nested calls handled). Fails (QSEM001) if the target measures or resets.
- `Controlled G(ctrl, ...args);` ‚Äî adds a control qubit (FIRST argument). Built-in gates only,
  never on user operations (QSEM002).

## Name resolution (C#/Q# rules)

For an unqualified call inside namespace NS, in order:
1. NS's own operations; 2. the global namespace; 3. the `open`ed namespaces ‚Äî exactly one match
wins, two+ is error QSEM018 (qualify to fix), zero falls through to QSEM007.
- Qualified names (`MyLib.Bell`) resolve directly. `open` is NOT transitive and does NOT load
  files (import does). Namespaces merge across files; the same operation name twice in one
  namespace is QSEM022 (in the global namespace, QSEM008).
- Built-in gates live in the implicit namespace `Qora.Intrinsic` (always open). A NAMESPACED
  operation may reuse a gate name (`namespace L { operation Rx ... }`); an unqualified use that
  could mean both is QSEM018 ‚Äî write `L.Rx(...)` or `Qora.Intrinsic.Rx(...)`. Global operations
  may NOT take gate names (QSEM013). Reserved everywhere: `M`, `Measure`, `measure`, `pi`, `tau`,
  `euler`, and declaring namespace `Qora.Intrinsic`.

## Error codes (collect-all; every error carries a source span)

QSEM001 Adjoint target not invertible (measures/resets, reason chained through calls)
QSEM002 Controlled on a user operation        QSEM003 functor on Reset/ResetAll
QSEM004 bare measurement statement            QSEM005 call inside an expression/condition/argument
QSEM006 wrong argument count/kind/register-size for a call
QSEM007 unknown gate/operation name           QSEM008 duplicate global operation name
QSEM009 calling the entry operation           QSEM010 entry operation has parameters
QSEM011 recursive operation calls             QSEM012 `use` outside entry op / inside loop/branch
QSEM013 name shadows a built-in where qualification cannot disambiguate
QSEM014 same qubit (or overlapping register/element) twice in one gate
QSEM015 duplicate declared names in one operation
QSEM016 literal qubit index out of range / indexing a single qubit
QSEM017 measurement assigned to a non-bit     QSEM018 ambiguous unqualified name (candidates listed)
QSEM019 unknown namespace/member in qualified name or `open`
QSEM020 import file not found/unreadable/no file context
QSEM021 import cycle (chain shown)            QSEM022 duplicate operation name within one namespace
QSEM023 names that would collide in the emitted program (e.g. `A.F` vs `A__F`; entry local vs def)
QSEM024 assignment to a `const` name (const = immutable binding; use `var`/`bit` for mutable)

## CLI contract (for tooling/agents)

```
qora --json [file] [--stages] [--base-dir <dir>]
```
- Source from `file` or stdin (UTF-8). Imports resolve next to `file`, or against `--base-dir`
  for stdin. Reply: ONE line of JSON:
  `{"success":bool,"qasm":"...","errors":[{"message","code","start","end"}]}`
  `start`/`end` are half-open character offsets into the entry document (-1 = no location).
- `--stages` adds `"ast"`, `"ir"`, `"irInverse"` (pipeline snapshots; original names, unmangled).
- Emitted QASM starts with `// generated by Qora v0.13`.

## Executing programs

- Full language (def/int/for/while): `python tools/run_braket.py file.qor --shots 1000` ‚Äî runs on
  Amazon Braket's LOCAL simulator (pip install amazon-braket-sdk, Python 3.10-3.13). Keep
  tools/stdgates.inc next to the working directory (the script handles this).
- Qiskit (`qiskit.qasm3.loads`): accepts only the FLAT subset ‚Äî no `def`, no `int` declarations,
  no variable qubit indices. Programs with operations/loops/variables will not load there today.

## Complete examples

Bell + measurement (minimal program):
```qora
operation Main() {
    use q = Qubit[2];
    H(q[0]);
    CNOT(q[0], q[1]);
    bit r0 = M(q[0]);
    bit r1 = M(q[1]);
}
```

Inverse synthesis (the flagship):
```qora
operation Prep(Qubit[2] q) {
    H(q[0]);
    T(q[1]);
    for i in 0..1 { X(q[i]); }
}
operation Main() {
    use q = Qubit[2];
    Prep(q);
    Adjoint Prep(q);      // compiler synthesizes Prep's inverse def
}
```

Multi-file (lib.qor + main.qor):
```qora
// lib.qor
namespace Lib {
    operation Flip(Qubit q) { X(q); }
}
// main.qor
import lib;
operation Main() {
    use q = Qubit[1];
    Lib.Flip(q[0]);
}
```

## Common mistakes to avoid

- `use` inside a non-entry operation or inside a loop/branch (QSEM012) ‚Äî allocate in Main, pass
  qubits as parameters.
- `if (M(q[0]) == 1)` (QSEM005) ‚Äî measure into a bit first, then test the bit.
- `Rx(q[0], pi/2)` (QSEM006) ‚Äî rotations take the ANGLE first.
- `qubit` lowercase as a type ‚Äî the keyword is `Qubit` (capital Q).
- Calling `Main` (QSEM009), giving `Main` parameters (QSEM010), recursion (QSEM011).
- Expecting `open` to load a file ‚Äî only `import` loads; `open` shortens names.
